title: LLM decoder q8 normalization frontier
hypothesis: The q8 PWL decoder path can replace exact normalization with a cheaper quantized reciprocal normalization while preserving the prompt-stress exact next-token and top-k gate.

direct_comparison.primary_question: Can q8 PWL reciprocal normalization at q10/q12/q14/q16 preserve the same prompt-stress quality as q8 exact normalization and the bf16 reciprocal anchor?
include: exact next-token and top-k quality gate
exclude: changing decoder RTL or mapper behavior
broad distribution robustness across new prompt sets
follow_on_broad_sweep: cost q8 reciprocal PWL against bf16 reciprocal PWL with a hardware-oriented block estimate
rerun broader prompt distribution robustness if q8 reciprocal becomes the selected implementation anchor

expected_benefit: tests the exact-normalization blocker isolated by the PWL frontier detail job
may move the immediate frontier from bf16 reciprocal PWL to a narrower q8 reciprocal PWL path
keeps the decision quality-gated instead of relying on cost proxy assumptions alone
risks: the prompt-stress set is still a pinned tiny decoder setup, not broad model acceptance
reciprocal precision that survives quality may still need RTL/OpenROAD cost confirmation
q8 reciprocal quality may be sensitive to prompt/logit distribution
